purpose: Cool hot oil flow using cooling water heat exchange, with degradation from water contamination affecting heat transfer efficiency

inputs.name: hot_oil_flow
inputs.description: Temperature of incoming hot oil to be cooled

outputs.name: cooled_oil_flow
outputs.description: Temperature of oil after cooling through heat exchanger

states.name: cooling_water_contamination
states.description: Chloride and sulphate contamination level in cooling water system

states.name: suspended_solids_buildup
states.description: Suspended solids and organics accumulation in cooling water system

states.name: heat_transfer_efficiency
states.description: Heat exchanger efficiency degraded by contamination and fouling

states.name: cooling_water_outlet_temp
states.description: Cooling water outlet temperature tracking heat load

events.name: cooling_water_outlet_overtemperature
events.description: Cooling water outlet temperature exceeds maximum allowable value

events.name: condensate_formation_during_unloading
events.description: Condensate forms during frequent unloading periods due to poor heat transfer

parameters.name: contamination_accumulation_rate
parameters.description: Rate of cooling water contamination buildup

parameters.name: efficiency_degradation_timescale
parameters.description: Time constant for heat transfer efficiency degradation

parameters.name: contamination_threshold
parameters.description: Contamination level that significantly affects efficiency

parameters.name: solids_threshold
parameters.description: Suspended solids level that significantly affects efficiency

parameters.name: nominal_cooling_capacity
parameters.description: Design temperature reduction at full efficiency

parameters.name: temp_tracking_coefficient
parameters.description: First-order lag coefficient for outlet temperature tracking

parameters.name: outlet_temp_threshold
parameters.description: Maximum allowable cooling water outlet temperature

parameters.name: efficiency_threshold
parameters.description: Minimum efficiency before condensate formation risk

state_transition_logic: cooling_water_contamination increases by contamination_accumulation_rate * dt, clamped to [0, inf]
suspended_solids_buildup increases by solids_accumulation_rate * dt, clamped to [0, inf]
heat_transfer_efficiency decreases by (1/efficiency_degradation_timescale) * contamination_driver * dt, clamped to [0, 1]
cooling_water_outlet_temp approaches target_outlet_temp using first-order lag with temp_tracking_coefficient, clamped to [0, 100]
assumptions: Heat transfer efficiency degrades proportionally to contamination levels
Cooling water outlet temperature follows heat load with thermal lag
Contamination and solids accumulate at constant rates
Oil cooling capacity is directly proportional to heat transfer efficiency
